channel.title: GeoPulse. Geopolitics in brief
channel.description: Signal-first briefings on geopolitics, edited by Lavkesh Dwivedi.

item.title: US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Offers To Open Strait Of Hormuz, Gives US Conditions
item.description: Iran offered to open the Strait of Hormuz and gave the US conditions for a ceasefire. US-Israel-Iran Ceasefire Live Updates: Iran Offers To Open Strait Of Hormuz, Gives US Conditions NDTV.

item.title: Box to launch Box Automate service to expedite enterprise business processes, CEO says
item.description: Box CEO announced the launch of Box Automate to expedite enterprise business processes. Box to launch Box Automate service to expedite enterprise business processes, CEO says.

item.title: Beyond the ‘iron rice bowl’: Why more young Chinese are choosing flexible work
item.description: One in three Chinese workers are in flexible jobs, with younger generations seeking autonomy. Around one in three workers in China are now in flexible jobs, led by a younger generation seeking greater autonomy despite the trade-offs.

item.title: China is remaking the South China Sea with a return to island-building
item.description: China is building a new outpost at Antelope Reef in the South China Sea. A new Chinese outpost at Antelope Reef won’t create legal rights but will shape control over disputed waters, says a maritime law professor from Western Sydney University.

item.title: Iran FM in Russia says US has offered new round of talks
item.description: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi met in Russia to discuss a possible new round of talks with the US. Russia is hosting Iran’s Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi in St. Petersburg for talks on a possible new round of talks.

item.title: China seeks to block US tech giant Meta from AI acquisition
item.description: China is blocking US tech giant Meta from an AI acquisition amid tightened scrutiny. Bejing tightens scrutiny of AI industry amid intensifying geopolitical rivalry with the US over the technology.

item.title: Iran Offers Plan to Focus on Strait of Hormuz and Delay Nuclear Talks
item.description: Iran proposed opening the Strait of Hormuz to shipping traffic and lifting the U. S. blockade in a new offer delivered on Sunday. The plan delays nuclear talks with the U. until later.

item.title: Canada’s New Route to Citizenship Has Thousands of Americans Lining Up to Apply
item.description: Canada's new citizenship route has thousands of Americans applying through ancestral ties. Canada has opened a route to citizenship for people who can prove they have a Canada-born ancestor. Millions could qualify, and Americans are already lining up to apply.

item.title: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrive for high-profile US state visit
item.description: King Charles and Queen Camilla arrived in the US for a state visit. The trip aims to strengthen ties amid tensions over the Iran conflict. The British monarch’s US visit underscores shared history amid strained ties and tensions over the Iran conflict.

item.title: Hezbollah and Israel swap threats and strikes across Lebanon’s border
item.description: Hezbollah and Israel exchanged threats and strikes across Lebanon's border, escalating tensions. Iran-linked group reiterates defiance; Israeli defence minister threatens to ‘burn all of Lebanon'. Hezbollah and Israel swap threats and strikes across Lebanon’s border.

item.title: Pompeii archaeologists use AI to reconstruct face of man who died during volcano eruption
item.description: Archaeologists used AI to reconstruct a man's face who died in Pompeii's 79AD eruption. The digital reconstruction is a first for the field. Archaeologists and researchers have used artificial intelligence for the first time to digitally reconstruct the face of a man killed in the eruption of Mount Vesuvius that engulfed the ancient Roman city of Pompeii in 79AD.

item.title: More than 100 migrants rescued in Channel less than week after £650m France deal
item.description: Over 100 migrants were rescued from the English Channel. The rescue occurred less than a week after the UK and France signed a £650m deal. More than 100 migrants have been rescued from the English Channel, less than a week after the UK signed a new deal with the French government to curb crossings.

item.title: Thousands held by paramilitary RSF in Sudan’s el-Fasher: NGO
item.description: The RSF paramilitary group is holding thousands of people in Sudan's el-Fasher. Hundreds of women and children are among the detained. Hundreds of women and children among those detained by the rebel group, says Sudan Doctors Network.

item.title: King Charles III arrives in U.S. on delicate mission to restore U.K.-U.S. relationship
item.description: King Charles III arrived in the US to restore the UK-US relationship. The President referred to him as a friend and great guy. The President has spoken in glowing terms about King Charles, repeatedly referring to the monarch as his “friend” and a “great guy”.

item.title: Trump Discusses Iran Offer To Open Straits Of Hormuz
item.description: Trump discussed Iran's proposal to open the Hormuz Strait with his national security team. US President Donald Trump on Monday met his national security team to discuss Iran's latest proposal to open the Hormuz Strait, the crucial waterway for transporting one-fifth of the global oil supplies.

item.title: Man charged with attempted assassination of Trump in White House correspondents' dinner shooting
item.description: A 31-year-old man was charged with attempted assassination of Trump at a White House correspondents' dinner.

item.title: Dave Nicholas joins The Times as Senior Vice President, Global Security
item.description: Dave Nicholas joined The New York Times Company as Senior Vice President of Global Security. He will oversee security operations. Dave Nicholas joins The Times as Senior Vice President, Global Security The New York Times Company.

item.title: At least four killed and dozens injured in Indonesia train crash
item.description: A commuter train and a long-distance train collided near Jakarta, killing four people and injuring dozens on Monday. Officials say a commuter train and a long-distance train collided on the outskirts of Jakarta on Monday.

item.title: United Nations Human Rights Committee Issues Favorable Decision in Support of Teymur Akhmedov
item.description: The United Nations Human Rights Committee issued a decision in support of Teymur Akhmedov. United Nations Human Rights Committee Issues Favorable Decision in Support of Teymur Akhmedov JW. ORG.

item.title: Freedom of navigation not negotiable: IMO addresses UN Security Council on maritime safety in
item.description: The International Maritime Organization Secretary-General said freedom of navigation is not negotiable, warning that any deviation from established principles would undermine shipping operations worldwide, setting a negative precedent and destabilizing global shipping.
